help.title: Integration Help
help.subtitle: Learn how to configure Vercel and GitHub integrations for your project.

vercel.title: Deploy to Vercel
vercel.step1Desc: Log in to your Vercel account at vercel.com
vercel.step2Desc: Click on your profile avatar in the top-right corner and select "Settings".
vercel.step3Desc: In the left sidebar, click on "Tokens" under the "Security" section.
vercel.step4Desc: Click "Create" and give your token a name (e.g., "Pi Project"). Set the expiration as needed.
vercel.step5Desc: Once created, copy the token immediately. It will only be shown once.
vercel.step6Desc: Return to the Settings page, paste your token, and click Deploy.
vercel.noteDesc: Vercel tokens are personal access tokens. They allow deployments from any project linked to your Vercel account. Keep your token secure and never share it publicly.

github.title: GitHub
github.step1Desc: If you don't have one, create a new repository at github.com/new. Make sure it's either public or private with your account.
github.step2Desc: Go to GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Tokens (classic) → Generate new token.
github.step3Scope: repo — Full control of private repositories (to push and pull)
github.step4Desc: Copy the generated token. You'll need to paste it into Pi when connecting.
github.step5Desc: In the Settings page, enter your repository URL (e.g., https://github.com/username/repo) and the branch name (default: main).
github.step6Desc: Paste your token and click "Connect GitHub". Once connected, you can push code changes directly from the editor toolbar.
github.securityDesc: Your token is stored locally and only used to authenticate with GitHub. It is never sent to our servers. If you believe your token has been compromised, revoke it immediately from GitHub Settings.
